video.title: The Most Polite Outlaws in History

character_bible.name: Host
character_bible.description: small deadpan stick figure host with a plain round pale face, tiny black dot eyes, thin black mustache, black bowler hat, slim black stick limbs, minimal and dry

character_bible.name: Claude Duval
character_bible.description: lean elegant French highwayman in a plum velvet coat with white lace cuffs, long dark curled hair under a tricorn hat, small black half-mask, graceful posture

character_bible.name: Black Bart
character_bible.description: older gentleman bandit with a thick white walrus mustache and bushy brows, long grey duster coat, dark derby hat, carries an old shotgun he never raises, unhurried

character_bible.name: Bill Miner
character_bible.description: aging outlaw with swept-back grey hair and a heavy grey mustache, worn dark suit, dried-blood red bandana knotted at his neck, tired kind eyes

scenes.narration: The most feared robbers of three centuries shared one rule. *Manners*.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a small bowler-hat stick figure host tipping his hat politely, plain warm cream background

scenes.narration: A highwayman who danced with his victims.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot, a masked highwayman in a plum coat bowing to an elegant lady beside a stopped stagecoach on a moonlit road

scenes.narration: A stagecoach bandit who left *poems* behind.
scenes.image_prompt: detail shot of a folded paper note weighted by a stone inside an open empty strongbox on a dusty road, deserted, nobody around

scenes.narration: A train robber famous for saying please.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot, an aging grey-mustached outlaw with a red bandana raising one polite gloved hand beside a halted steam locomotive at night

scenes.narration: All three were loved. All three were hunted.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot, a smiling crowd of pale faces on one side and grim lawmen holding rifles on the other, the small bowler-hat host standing in the gap between them

scenes.narration: So did courtesy ever save an outlaw's neck?
scenes.image_prompt: detail shot of a hangman's noose hanging beside a white lace glove with a dried-blood red ribbon, on a wooden beam, nobody around

scenes.narration: Story one. The gentleman of the London roads.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot of the bowler-hat host holding up one finger, plain warm cream background

scenes.narration: Claude Duval, a footman from Normandy, arrives in England in 1660.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot, a young man in plain servant livery stepping off a ship gangplank carrying a small trunk, busy grey harbor behind

scenes.narration: He serves a duke by day and studies wealthy travelers by night.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot, the young footman holding a candlestick at a tall window of a grand hallway, watching a gilded coach pass below in the dark

scenes.narration: Then he buys a horse, a mask, and a velvet coat.
scenes.image_prompt: detail shot of a plum velvet coat laid on a wooden table beside a black half-mask, riding gloves and a single dried-blood red rose, nobody around

scenes.narration: By 1668, Claude Duval is the most wanted highwayman in England.
scenes.image_prompt: very wide shot, a caped rider on horseback on a moonlit hilltop overlooking a winding coaching road

scenes.narration: His hunting ground is the road from Highgate into London.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of an empty tree-lined coaching road at dusk, deep wheel ruts, low mist, deserted, nobody around

scenes.narration: But Duval robs like no one else on that road.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot, the masked highwayman sweeping his tricorn hat into a deep theatrical bow beside a stopped coach

scenes.narration: He reputedly never uses violence. Not once.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot on hands, the highwayman gently placing a small locket back into a lady's open gloved palm

scenes.narration: One night, the legend says, he stops a knight's coach.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot, coach lanterns glowing in dark woods, horses steaming, a masked figure holding the bridle

scenes.narration: Inside sit a wealthy knight, a locked strongbox, and his young wife.
scenes.image_prompt: medium interior shot of a coach, a nervous gentleman clutching a strongbox on his lap, an elegant calm lady seated beside him

scenes.narration: Duval opens the door. And asks the lady to dance.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot, the masked highwayman holding the coach door open and extending a gloved hand toward the lady inside

scenes.narration: They dance a courante on the roadside, by lamplight.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot, the highwayman and the lady mid-dance on moonlit roadside grass, coach lamps glowing, her dried-blood red sash swirling, coachmen staring

scenes.narration: Her husband watches from the coach. Politely.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of the husband's strained smiling face framed in the coach window, knuckles white on the sill

scenes.narration: For the dance, Duval takes only *part* of the money.
scenes.image_prompt: detail shot of gloved hands returning a share of coins into the open strongbox, lamplight, close on the coins

scenes.narration: The story spreads through every tavern in London.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of a warm crowded tavern, drinkers leaning over tankards toward one animated storyteller, lamplight and smoke

scenes.narration: It is said some ladies hoped their coach would be next.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot, two elegant ladies peering eagerly out of a coach window into a dark wooded road

scenes.narration: The king's men do not find it charming.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot, stern officers in long coats nailing a rough charcoal portrait sketch to a wooden post, no readable text

scenes.narration: A price lands on Claude Duval's head.
scenes.image_prompt: detail shot of a heavy leather purse of coins set on a magistrate's desk beside a charcoal portrait sketch, candlelight, nobody around

scenes.narration: January 1670. Soldiers surround the Hole in the Wall tavern.
scenes.image_prompt: wide night shot, soldiers with lanterns and muskets converging on a narrow glowing tavern doorway in Covent Garden

scenes.narration: Duval is taken at the table, without a single shot.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot, soldiers seizing the elegant highwayman mid-toast, his tankard spilling dark red wine across the table

scenes.narration: A judge convicts him of *six* robberies.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot from below, a stern wigged judge peering down from a high bench at the masked man in the dock

scenes.narration: The story goes that noble ladies begged the king for mercy.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot, three elegant ladies kneeling with clasped hands before a distant throne in a vast palace hall

scenes.narration: King Charles the Second says no.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a king in dark curls turning his face away, one ringed hand raised flat in refusal

scenes.narration: On January 21st, 1670, Duval hangs at Tyburn. He is 27.
scenes.image_prompt: very wide shot, a distant gallows silhouette against a grey dawn sky, a large silent crowd below, muted

scenes.narration: London buries him under a stone in Covent Garden.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of a candlelit church interior, a coffin covered in pale flowers, well-dressed mourners with bowed heads

scenes.narration: His epitaph warns men to guard their purses. And women, their hearts.
scenes.image_prompt: detail shot of a worn stone memorial slab in a church floor with a single dried-blood red rose laid on it, nobody around

scenes.narration: Story two. The poet of the stagecoach lines.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot of the bowler-hat host holding up two fingers, plain warm cream background

scenes.narration: Two centuries later. Northern California, 1875.
scenes.image_prompt: very wide shot of golden dry hills under a big sky, a dusty stage road winding through, deserted, nobody around

scenes.narration: Wells Fargo coaches carry gold through these hills every week.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot, a six-horse stagecoach with a strongbox on the roof cresting a golden hill, dust trailing

scenes.narration: Charles Boles, an English-born drifter, has other plans for that gold.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot, an older man with a white walrus mustache watching a distant coach from a ridgeline, coat flapping

scenes.narration: In July 1875, near Copperopolis, a figure steps onto the road.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot, a man in a long grey duster standing calmly in the center of a dirt road, shotgun held low and pointed at nothing

scenes.narration: He says please. The driver, reasonably, hands over the box.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot, a stagecoach driver tossing a wooden strongbox down to the courteous grey bandit waiting with open arms

scenes.narration: The take is *one hundred sixty* dollars. A career is born.
scenes.image_prompt: detail shot of an opened strongbox holding a modest pile of coins and a few banknotes, dust motes in the light, nobody around

scenes.narration: Two years later, he starts leaving something behind at the scenes.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of an emptied express box abandoned on a roadside at dusk, a small pale paper square resting on it, deserted

scenes.narration: He leaves poems.
scenes.image_prompt: detail shot of a folded paper note weighted by a small stone on the wooden box, one dried-blood red wax dot on the fold, nobody around

scenes.narration: Signed, Black Bart, the P O 8.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a folded note with a small confident ink flourish at its corner, blurred and unreadable, on dark wood

scenes.narration: One reads, I've labored long and hard for bread, for honor and for riches.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot, a weary man swinging a pick on a barren hillside claim, bent back, sweat, harsh light

scenes.narration: But on my corns too long you've tread, you fine-haired sons of bitches.
scenes.image_prompt: medium caricature shot, fat financiers in tall top hats and fur collars stepping carelessly over tiny bowed workers

scenes.narration: The newspapers love him. Wells Fargo does not.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot of a detective's office wall covered in maps and pinned notes, a hard-worn desk below, nobody around

scenes.narration: Over eight years, Black Bart robs at least *28* coaches.
scenes.image_prompt: very wide shot of a winding golden road with several distant stagecoaches at different bends, tiny in the landscape

scenes.narration: He works on foot. Because Black Bart is afraid of horses.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot, the grey bandit eyeing a saddled horse warily from several steps away, the horse eyeing him back

scenes.narration: He simply walks away from every robbery. Every single one.
scenes.image_prompt: very wide shot, a lone figure in a duster walking unhurried into vast golden hills, tiny in the frame

scenes.narration: In eight years, he reportedly never fires his shotgun once.
scenes.image_prompt: detail shot of an old shotgun propped untouched against a tree stump, wildflowers growing around the stock, nobody around

scenes.narration: Drivers describe grey hair, a polite voice, and no violence.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot, a stagecoach driver gesturing calmly while a lawman listens, both unbothered, dusty depot

scenes.narration: November 1883. Back at Copperopolis, where it all began.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of the same dirt hill road in colder autumn light, bare trees, long shadows, deserted, nobody around

scenes.narration: The same road. The same man. One dropped *handkerchief*.
scenes.image_prompt: detail shot of a white handkerchief lying crumpled on the dirt road, wind lifting one corner, nobody around

scenes.narration: In its corner, a tiny laundry mark. F X O 7.
scenes.image_prompt: extreme close shot of the handkerchief corner showing a tiny smudged ink stamp, threads visible, blurred and unreadable

scenes.narration: Detectives carry that mark through San Francisco's laundries, one by one.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot, a detective holding the cloth up at a steamy laundry counter while a laundryman squints at it

scenes.narration: It leads to a boarding house. And a quiet gentleman named Bolton.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot, a neat older gentleman in a grey suit and derby descending boarding-house steps with a cane, serene

scenes.narration: Charles Bolton, respectable mining man, is Black Bart the poet.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of the gentleman's calm face, tired eyes, the faintest amused line under the white mustache

scenes.narration: He is polite in court. Naturally. He goes to prison.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot, the old bandit standing straight before a judge, derby hat held to his chest, chin level

scenes.narration: Released in 1888, reporters ask if he will rob coaches again.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot, a scrum of reporters in bowler hats crowding prison steps around one calm grey figure

scenes.narration: No, gentlemen. I am through with crime. That is a *quote*.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot, the old bandit tipping his derby with a small dry smile

scenes.narration: Weeks later, Charles Boles walks out of his hotel and vanishes.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of a foggy San Francisco street at dawn, one dark figure walking away with his back turned, dissolving into the fog

scenes.narration: No grave. No body. Just twenty-eight robberies and two poems.
scenes.image_prompt: detail shot of two folded paper notes and a brass strongbox key arranged on dark cloth, museum-quiet, nobody around

scenes.narration: Story three. The Grey Fox.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot of the bowler-hat host holding up three fingers, plain warm cream background

scenes.narration: Bill Miner starts robbing stagecoaches in California as a teenager.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot, a young rider reining up beside a stagecoach at dusk, hand extended almost apologetically

scenes.narration: He is known on the roads for one strange habit. Courtesy.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot, the young outlaw collecting valuables in his upturned hat with a small courteous bow to seated passengers

scenes.narration: Some say he is the first man to ever say, hands up.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot inside a coach, passengers' gloved hands all raised against the window light

scenes.narration: The habit keeps buying him decades inside San Quentin prison.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of a vast stone prison yard, one man sweeping alone in a long diagonal of light

scenes.narration: He walks out in 1901, fifty-four years old and out of date.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot, an aged grey-mustached man stepping through a heavy iron gate into blinding daylight, holding a small bundle

scenes.narration: Stagecoaches are gone. The world has moved on to trains.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of a rusting stagecoach sunk in weeds beside gleaming new rail tracks, deserted, nobody around

scenes.narration: So Bill Miner moves on to trains too.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot, the old outlaw on a hillside watching a locomotive thunder past below, coat and red bandana snapping in its wind

scenes.narration: He crosses into Canada under a new name. George Edwards.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot, a quiet rancher walking a fence line with a bucket, huge dusty blue mountains behind

scenes.narration: In British Columbia, neighbors know him as a gentle old rancher.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot, the old rancher feeding an apple to a farm horse over a rail fence, soft morning light

scenes.narration: In 1904, masked men stop a train at Silverdale.
scenes.image_prompt: wide night shot, a lantern-lit locomotive halted on the track, three masked silhouettes standing in the steam

scenes.narration: It is widely called the first train robbery in Canadian history.
scenes.image_prompt: detail shot of white steam curling around the locomotive's dark driving wheels, night, nobody visible

scenes.narration: No one is ever conclusively tied to it. Officially.
scenes.image_prompt: detail shot of an empty wooden interrogation chair under a single hanging lamp in a dark room, nobody around

scenes.narration: Two years later, near Monte Creek, Miner stops another train.
scenes.image_prompt: medium night shot, the old masked outlaw climbing into a mail car, lantern light catching his red bandana

scenes.narration: This time the take is *fifteen dollars*. And someone's kidney pills.
scenes.image_prompt: detail shot of a small open safe holding a few coins and one medicine bottle with a dried-blood red label, nobody around

scenes.narration: The Mounties catch the Grey Fox within days.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot, red-coated Mounties on horseback circling one weary old man standing calm in dry grass

scenes.narration: He goes to the British Columbia Penitentiary. Briefly.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of grey penitentiary walls under low cloud, a tiny barred window catching one square of light, nobody around

scenes.narration: In 1907, Bill Miner escapes. Canada never catches him again.
scenes.image_prompt: detail shot of a fresh hole dug under a wooden stockade wall at dawn, scattered earth, empty guard tower above

scenes.narration: He is past sixty. He is not finished.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot, an old man's silhouette walking south along rail tracks into a pale dawn, small bundle over his shoulder

scenes.narration: He is caught in Georgia. He escapes. Twice.
scenes.image_prompt: low angle detail shot of worn boots splashing through dark swamp water, reeds blurring past

scenes.narration: The last escape leads through a swamp. He drinks the water.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of still black swamp water under hanging moss, one line of ripples crossing it, deserted, nobody around

scenes.narration: It kills him slowly. He dies in a prison bed in 1913.
scenes.image_prompt: detail shot of barred window light falling on an empty made cot, a folded dried-blood red bandana on the pillow, nobody around

scenes.narration: Three outlaws. Three centuries. One shared weapon. Please and thank you.
scenes.image_prompt: detail shot of three objects in a row on warm cream cloth, a black half-mask, a folded paper poem, a red bandana, nobody around

scenes.narration: The crowds loved every one of them.
scenes.image_prompt: wide soft shot of a faded cheering crowd in period clothes, hats raised, warm sepia light

scenes.narration: The law hanged one, jailed one, and outlasted the third.
scenes.image_prompt: detail shot of a wooden gavel at rest on its block, dust motes in a courtroom shaft of light, nobody around

scenes.narration: Their manners bought them fame. Fame paid for nothing.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot, the bowler-hat host removing his hat and holding it to his chest, head slightly bowed, plain warm cream background

scenes.narration: Being loved was never the same as being spared.
scenes.image_prompt: detail shot of the hangman's noose and white lace glove from before, now with a single dried-blood red rose lying beneath them, nobody around
